vendors.name: Oracle Aconex
vendors.tagline: Document control & project collaboration for mega-projects
vendors.market_position: The document-control and collaboration standard on large UAE projects (Oracle runs a dedicated .ae site; users include Dubai Airports and major EPC contractors). Built for the multi-party coordination big builds demand.
pros: Deep document, RFI and submittal control
Neutral, auditable record across all parties
vendors.ae_specific_note: A backbone for main contractors and PMCs on large jobs, not a back-office tool — pair it with a VAT-compliant accounting system.

vendors.name: Oracle Primavera P6
vendors.tagline: Critical-path scheduling & cost control for large projects
vendors.market_position: The de-facto scheduling and project-controls tool on major UAE projects, sold through long-established Dubai resellers and usually paired with Aconex.
pros: Often mandated by government / EPC clients
vendors.ae_specific_note: Government and large private clients frequently mandate Primavera-format programmes — often a contractual requirement, not a choice.

vendors.name: Autodesk Construction Cloud
vendors.tagline: BIM, design collaboration & construction management
vendors.market_position: Central to Dubai’s BIM mandate (BIM model submission required for many new permits since Jan 2024) and used across major UAE developments. The BIM + construction-management standard.
cons: BIM depth wasted on simple jobs
vendors.ae_specific_note: If you build in Dubai above the BIM thresholds (or any government project), BIM submission to ISO 19650 / IFC is required — Autodesk is the default environment.

vendors.name: Procore
vendors.tagline: All-in-one construction project management
vendors.market_position: Opened its MENA office in Dubai (Internet City), backed by Abu Dhabi’s Mubadala. A strong, well-designed project platform for mid-to-large UAE builds — but lighter on local compliance than the UAE-native ERPs.
vendors.ae_specific_note: Procore handles project management, not UAE VAT — you’ll still need an FTA-compliant accounting tool (First Bit, Focus, Zoho Books) alongside it.

vendors.name: Bentley Systems
vendors.tagline: Infrastructure BIM & 4D construction modelling
vendors.market_position: Infrastructure-grade BIM (ProjectWise, SYNCHRO, OpenRoads) with a Dubai office, used across UAE highways, utilities and large civil works.
vendors.ae_specific_note: Most relevant for infrastructure and civil packages rather than standard building fit-out.

vendors.name: PlanRadar
vendors.tagline: Site inspections, snagging & defect management
vendors.market_position: Runs its MENA HQ in Dubai — the most accessible mid-market site-execution tool in the UAE, with Arabic support and one of the few published price cards.
pros: Affordable per-user model with a free trial
Strong for snagging, QA and handover
vendors.typical_pricing: Published (global USD card): ~$35 Basic / ~$119 Starter / ~$179 Pro per user/mo; Enterprise custom.
vendors.ae_specific_note: The strongest entry point for UAE SMB contractors who want digital snagging and inspections without an enterprise rollout.

vendors.name: First Bit ERP
vendors.tagline: Construction ERP: estimating, cost control & accounting
vendors.market_position: A UAE-focused construction ERP (Dubai support team) — estimation, cost control, retention and accounting, FTA-approved for VAT since 2017 and now handling corporate tax.
vendors.ae_specific_note: The best "one local differentiator" pick in the UAE: a construction ERP already built around FTA VAT and Arabic, rather than a US tool bolted onto separate invoicing.

vendors.name: Focus Softnet
vendors.tagline: ERP/CRM with construction project costing
vendors.market_position: A Dubai-rooted ERP house operating in the UAE since 1995 with 6,000+ local customers — UAE-VAT-compliant ERP/CRM with construction project costing and CAFM.
cons: Broad suite — more than small firms need
vendors.ae_specific_note: Strong local install base and support; shortlist it against First Bit and PACT on construction-specific depth.

vendors.name: PACT ERP
vendors.tagline: Contracting ERP: BOQ, procurement & payroll
vendors.market_position: A regional contracting ERP with a Dubai office (Silicon Oasis) — BOQ, project costing, procurement and payroll, UAE-VAT-compliant.
cons: Smaller ecosystem than the global tools
vendors.ae_specific_note: A solid contracting-ERP alternative for UAE firms that want project costing plus FTA-ready accounting in one system.

vendors.name: Sage (200 / 300)
vendors.tagline: Mid-market ERP/accounting fitted to UAE construction cost control
vendors.market_position: A global mid-market ERP widely deployed for UAE construction cost control via Sage Middle East and many Dubai/Abu Dhabi partners, with native UAE VAT.
cons: Construction depth depends on the partner fit-out
vendors.ae_specific_note: Best where finance and cost control are the priority and a local partner tailors it to your contracting workflow.

vendors.name: Zoho (Projects / Books / FSM)
vendors.tagline: Affordable PM + accounting + field service for SMB trades
vendors.market_position: The pragmatic SMB stack — Projects for management, Books (FTA-listed) for UAE VAT accounting, and FSM for field service — popular with smaller UAE trades and contracting firms.
pros: Zoho Books is FTA-accredited for UAE VAT
cons: Project depth lighter than a true construction ERP
vendors.typical_pricing: Published per-user tiers (USD) on zoho.com; Books has a free tier.
vendors.ae_specific_note: The cheapest compliant on-ramp for small UAE contractors: Zoho Books keeps you FTA-VAT-correct, with Projects/FSM layered on as you grow.

vendors.name: Horizon FMS (FIT)
vendors.tagline: UAE CAFM / facilities management — assets, PPM, work orders
vendors.market_position: A UAE-built CAFM/facilities-management platform for FM and maintenance contractors — assets, PPM, work orders and contracts, with UAE VAT.
vendors.ae_specific_note: A locally-built option for UAE facilities-management and MEP-maintenance firms that want CAFM with local support.

vendors.name: FSM Grid
vendors.tagline: Field-service management for MEP & maintenance
vendors.market_position: A field-service platform with an active Dubai/UAE presence, frequently listed among top FSM tools in the Emirates for dispatch and work-order management.
pros: Good fit for MEP, HVAC and FM contractors
cons: Arabic / local support — confirm with vendor
vendors.ae_specific_note: Native Arabic and VAT support weren’t independently confirmed — verify with the vendor before relying on them for compliant invoicing.

vendors.name: Dubai Municipality (Contractor Licensing)
vendors.tagline: Contractor classification, licensing & building permits in Dubai
vendors.market_position: The Dubai authority for contractor classification, licensing and building permits. Dubai Law No. 7 of 2025 (in force Jan 2026) introduces a mandatory central Contractor Register and capability-based classification.
pros: Contractor classification + building permits in Dubai
New central Contractor Register under Dubai Law No. 7 of 2025
Linked to the Invest in Dubai platform
cons: Dubai Law 7/2025 compliance deadline by Jan 2027
Permits must meet Dubai building standards + BIM
vendors.ae_specific_note: As of mid-2026, existing Dubai contractors must comply with the new registration/classification regime by Jan 2027 — keep your registration current.

vendors.name: Ministry of Energy & Infrastructure (MOEI)
vendors.tagline: Federal registration & e-qualification of contractors
vendors.market_position: The federal body that registers and e-qualifies consultants, contractors and suppliers for federal engineering projects across the UAE.
vendors.ae_specific_note: UAE compliance splits federal (MOEI) vs emirate (Dubai Municipality / Abu Dhabi DMT) — you may need registration at more than one level.

vendors.name: UAE VAT (Federal Tax Authority)
vendors.tagline: VAT at 5% — administered by the FTA since 2018
vendors.market_position: The Federal Tax Authority administers UAE VAT at a flat 5% (live since 2018). Construction services are generally standard-rated, so your accounting tool must handle 5% VAT and FTA filing.
vendors.ae_specific_note: A Saudi-tuned (15%) product will mis-rate every UAE invoice — choose software set up for UAE’s 5% and FTA returns (First Bit, Focus, Sage, Zoho Books).

vendors.name: UAE E-Invoicing (Peppol)
vendors.tagline: Decentralised Peppol model — phasing in from 2026
vendors.market_position: Unlike Saudi’s live ZATCA clearance model, UAE e-invoicing uses a decentralised Peppol approach and isn’t mandatory yet — pilot in 2026, large taxpayers (≥AED 50M) from Jan 2027, others through 2027.
pros: Time to prepare before the mandate
cons: NOT mandatory yet (large taxpayers from Jan 2027)
Different model from Saudi’s ZATCA clearance
vendors.ae_specific_note: As of mid-2026 you don’t need e-invoicing to operate, but buy software that is "Peppol-ready" so you’re covered when the mandate lands.

vendors.name: MOHRE & Emiratisation (Nafis)
vendors.tagline: Federal labour rules + Emiratisation quotas
vendors.market_position: The federal labour regulator enforces Emiratisation quotas (with steep per-position fines) while the Nafis programme subsidises Emirati hiring. Payroll/HR software must track Emirati ratios and integrate WPS.
pros: Centralises labour contracts and work permits
Nafis offers salary subsidies for hiring Emiratis
vendors.ae_specific_note: Emiratisation is the UAE equivalent of Saudi’s Nitaqat — a different ruleset, so UAE payroll tools must track Emirati headcount, not Saudi GOSI banding.

vendors.name: Abu Dhabi DMT & Estidama
vendors.tagline: Abu Dhabi permits + mandatory Estidama Pearl rating
vendors.market_position: Abu Dhabi’s Department of Municipalities & Transport runs building permits and the mandatory Estidama Pearl sustainability rating (minimum 1 Pearl for all buildings, 2 Pearl for government) — required to get a permit.
pros: Abu Dhabi building permits and regulation
Clear minimum standards (1 Pearl all, 2 Pearl government)
cons: Estidama Pearl is a permit gate
vendors.ae_specific_note: If you work across Dubai and Abu Dhabi, your workflows and software often need per-emirate configuration — the UAE is not a single national stack.

vendors.name: Dubai BIM Mandate
vendors.tagline: BIM model submission required for many Dubai permits
vendors.market_position: Dubai Municipality requires BIM model submission for new building permits above set thresholds (since Jan 2024), to ISO 19650 / IFC — driving BIM adoption down the supply chain.
pros: BIM submission required for larger Dubai permits since Jan 2024
Covers hospitals, universities, all government projects
vendors.ae_specific_note: If you design or sub-contract on qualifying Dubai projects, BIM-capable tools (Autodesk, Bentley) aren’t optional — the permit depends on it.
